pattern: Each digit multiplies the set of partial strings by its letters, so a backtracking (or iterative product) build enumerates every combination.
hints: Map each digit to its letters, then think about how adding one more digit transforms the set of strings so far.
Build the combinations one digit at a time: every existing partial string gets extended by each letter the next digit offers.
Use backtracking with an index into digits: at each level loop over that digit's letters, append one, recurse to the next index, and record the string when the index reaches the end.

edge_inputs.description: single digit maps to three letters
a four-letter digit gives four options
two four-letter digits multiply to sixteen
repeated digit still produces all letter pairs

stress.description: random digit strings of length 1 to 4, biased toward four-letter digits
stress.note: length capped at four per constraints; covers four-letter digits 7 and 9
